description: Move hardcoded secrets (BOT_TOKEN, ADMIN_ID, DATABASE_URL) to .env file using python-dotenv. Create .env and .env.example files.
steps: Create .env file in project root with: BOT_TOKEN=REDACTED, ADMIN_ID=REDACTED, DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:REDACTED@localhost/utilities_db
Create .env.example file with placeholder values: BOT_TOKEN=your_bot_token_here, ADMIN_ID=your_admin_id_here, DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost/utilities_db

Modify main.py: add 'from dotenv import load_dotenv' and 'load_dotenv()' at the top (after standard imports). Replace hardcoded BOT_TOKEN with os.getenv('BOT_TOKEN') and ADMIN_ID with int(os.getenv('ADMIN_ID')). The 'import os' is already present.
Modify database.py: add 'import os' and 'from dotenv import load_dotenv' and 'load_dotenv()' at the top. Replace hardcoded DATABASE_URL with os.getenv('DATABASE_URL').
Add .env to .gitignore file (create .gitignore if it doesn't exist)
acceptance_criteria: .env file exists with actual token values
Both files import and call load_dotenv()
All .py files pass Python syntax validation
verification: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('main.py').read()); ast.parse(open('database.py').read()); print('Syntax OK')"
grep -q 'load_dotenv' main.py && echo 'main.py loads dotenv' || echo 'MISSING load_dotenv in main.py'
grep -q 'load_dotenv' database.py && echo 'database.py loads dotenv' || echo 'MISSING load_dotenv in database.py'
findings: .env file is correctly git-ignored so it was not staged. The .env file exists on disk with real values for local use. All syntax checks pass.
